title: Firefly: The Game - Space is the Place to Work a Job (Review)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eOxOgo6VNzQ

Daniel (@dungeondive) dives into Firefly the board game, a blue-collar space Western that scratches the sandbox itch. This isn't about combat—it's about finding a crew, picking up cargo, delivering it across a dangerous solar system, and hopefully keeping everyone alive. The core gameplay loop is satisfyingly straightforward: build your crew through a hiring mechanic that also assigns salaries, move through space with a delicious press-your-luck element (rolling encounter cards while burning fuel), and work jobs for various factions to build relationships and bank credits. Daniel particularly loves the movement system, where every full burn risks triggering random encounters from the Alliance or worse. The solo mode is lean but functional, with multiple objective paths (solid reputation with all factions, accumulating credits, or completing misbehavior cards for a wild-card challenge). The expansions add scenarios and variety without inflating the price—a solid deal if you find the base game with a couple expansions for around $60.

What's really cool is the rich vein of fan-made content available. The community has created alternate solo scenarios and NPC rules, proving that this sandbox template is genuinely welcoming to house rules and expansion. The ships themselves are admittedly the weakest mechanical element, but if you're hunting for games like Wandering Galaxy, Spacers, or Outer Rim—games where the joy is in scrappy survival and working the edges of the system—Firefly deserves serious consideration.

----

What's your favorite blue-collar space adventure game, and what would push Firefly into your collection?
